pull_request.title: feat(pygal): implement funnel-meta-analysis
pull_request.body:
## Implementation: `funnel-meta-analysis` - pygal

Implements the **pygal** version of `funnel-meta-analysis`.

**File:** `plots/funnel-meta-analysis/implementations/pygal.py`

**Parent Issue:** #4590

---
:robot: *[impl-generate workflow](https://github.com/MarkusNeusinger/pyplots/actions/runs/23114663070)*

changed_files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,176 @@
+""" pyplots.ai
+funnel-meta-analysis: Meta-Analysis Funnel Plot for Publication Bias
+Library: pygal 3.1.0 | Python 3.14.3
+Quality: 90/100 | Created: 2026-03-15
+"""
+
+import numpy as np
+import pygal
+from pygal.style import Style
+
+
+# Data: 15 RCTs comparing drug vs placebo (log odds ratios)
+np.random.seed(42)
+study_names = [
+    "Adams 2018",
+    "Baker 2019",
+    "Chen 2019",
+    "Davis 2020",
+    "Evans 2020",
+    "Foster 2021",
+    "Garcia 2021",
+    "Harris 2022",
+    "Ibrahim 2022",
+    "Jones 2022",
+    "Kim 2023",
+    "Lee 2023",
+    "Martinez 2023",
+    "Nelson 2024",
+    "O'Brien 2024",
+]
+
+# Effect sizes (log odds ratios) and standard errors
+# High-precision studies cluster near pooled effect; low-precision studies
+# show rightward asymmetry (missing negative small studies = publication bias)
+# Martinez 2023 (index 12) is clearly outside CI, demonstrating an outlier
+effect_sizes = np.array(
+    [-0.50, -0.42, -0.68, -0.46, -0.25, -0.55, -0.48, -0.44, -0.38, -0.20, -0.72, -0.43, -0.08, -0.51, -0.58]
+)
+std_errors = np.array([0.08, 0.11, 0.17, 0.09, 0.16, 0.21, 0.13, 0.07, 0.22, 0.18, 0.26, 0.10, 0.23, 0.12, 0.24])
+
+# Summary (pooled) effect size
+pooled_effect = -0.47
+
+# Separate studies by precision for visual hierarchy
+high_precision_mask = std_errors < 0.15
+low_precision_mask = ~high_precision_mask
+
+# Publication-quality style with refined typography and palette
+custom_style = Style(
+    background="#FFFFFF",
+    plot_background="#F8FAFB",
+    foreground="#2B3A4A",
+    foreground_strong="#1A2636",
+    foreground_subtle="#E4E8EC",
+    colors=(
+        "#6FA8C7",  # 0: CI left boundary (muted teal-blue)
+        "#6FA8C7",  # 1: CI right boundary (matched)
+        "#1A2636",  # 2: Pooled effect line (dark navy)
+        "#9CA8B4",  # 3: Null effect line (muted grey)
+        "#306998",  # 4: High-precision studies (Python blue)
+        "#D95F02",  # 5: Low-precision studies (strong orange, colorblind-safe)
+    ),
+    title_font_size=48,
+    label_font_size=28,
+    major_label_font_size=24,
+    legend_font_size=22,
+    value_font_size=18,
+    tooltip_font_size=22,
+    stroke_width=3,
+    font_family="Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif",
+    opacity=".9",
+    opacity_hover="1",
+)
+
+# Custom y-axis labels for clean tick positioning
+y_labels_list = [0.00, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30]
+
+# Chart with refined configuration
+chart = pygal.XY(
+    width=4800,
+    height=2700,
+    title="funnel-meta-analysis \u00b7 pygal \u00b7 pyplots.ai",
+    x_title="Log Odds Ratio (Effect Size)",
+    y_title="Standard Error (precision \u2191)",
+    style=custom_style,
+    show_legend=True,
+    legend_at_bottom=True,
+    legend_at_bottom_columns=3,
+    legend_box_size=20,
+    dots_size=14,
+    stroke=False,
+    show_y_guides=True,
+    show_x_guides=False,
+    margin=50,
+    inverse_y_axis=True,
+    truncate_legend=-1,
+    x_value_formatter=lambda x: f"{x:.2f}",
+    y_value_formatter=lambda y: f"{y:.2f}",
+    y_labels=y_labels_list,
+    print_values=False,
+    print_zeroes=False,
+    range=(0, 0.30),
+    xrange=(-1.10, 0.18),
+    spacing=25,
+    tooltip_border_radius=8,
+    explicit_size=True,
+)
+
+# Funnel boundaries (pseudo 95% CI) - left boundary
+se_values = np.linspace(0, 0.30, 60)
+funnel_left = [(float(pooled_effect - 1.96 * se), float(se)) for se in se_values]
+chart.add(
+    "95% Pseudo CI",
+    funnel_left,
+    stroke=True,
+    show_dots=False,
+    stroke_style={"width": 5, "dasharray": "14, 6"},
+    formatter=lambda x: "",
+)
+
+# Right CI boundary (suppressed from legend via None)
+funnel_right = [(float(pooled_effect + 1.96 * se), float(se)) for se in se_values]
+chart.add(
+    None,
+    funnel_right,
+    stroke=True,
+    show_dots=False,
+    stroke_style={"width": 5, "dasharray": "14, 6"},
+    formatter=lambda x: "",
+)
+
+# Vertical line at pooled effect (prominent, solid)
+chart.add(
+    f"Pooled Effect (LOR = {pooled_effect:.2f})",
+    [(float(pooled_effect), 0.0), (float(pooled_effect), 0.30)],
+    stroke=True,
+    show_dots=False,
+    stroke_style={"width": 7},
+    formatter=lambda x: "",
+)
+
+# Vertical dashed line at null effect (0)
+chart.add(
+    "Null Effect (LOR = 0)",
+    [(0.0, 0.0), (0.0, 0.30)],
+    stroke=True,
+    show_dots=False,
+    stroke_style={"width": 3, "dasharray": "10, 8"},
+    formatter=lambda x: "",
+)
+
+# High-precision studies (larger markers, tightly clustered near pooled effect)
+hp_points = []
+for name, es, se in zip(
+    np.array(study_names)[high_precision_mask],
+    effect_sizes[high_precision_mask],
+    std_errors[high_precision_mask],
+    strict=True,
+):
+    hp_points.append({"value": (float(es), float(se)), "label": f"{name}: LOR={es:.2f}, SE={se:.2f}"})
+chart.add("High-precision studies", hp_points, stroke=False, dots_size=22, formatter=lambda x: "")
+
+# Low-precision studies (medium markers, asymmetric scatter revealing bias)
+lp_points = []
+for name, es, se in zip(
+    np.array(study_names)[low_precision_mask],
+    effect_sizes[low_precision_mask],
+    std_errors[low_precision_mask],
+    strict=True,
+):
+    lp_points.append({"value": (float(es), float(se)), "label": f"{name}: LOR={es:.2f}, SE={se:.2f}"})
+chart.add("Low-precision studies (bias region)", lp_points, stroke=False, dots_size=16, formatter=lambda x: "")
+
+# Render outputs
+chart.render_to_png("plot.png", dpi=192)
+chart.render_to_file("plot.html")

@@ -0,0 +1,225 @@
+library: pygal
+specification_id: funnel-meta-analysis
+created: '2026-03-15T16:44:54Z'
+updated: '2026-03-15T17:15:33Z'
+generated_by: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
+workflow_run: 23114663070
+issue: 4590
+python_version: 3.14.3
+library_version: 3.1.0
+preview_url: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/funnel-meta-analysis/pygal/plot.png
+preview_thumb: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/funnel-meta-analysis/pygal/plot_thumb.png
+preview_html: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/funnel-meta-analysis/pygal/plot.html
+quality_score: 90
+review:
+  strengths:
+  - Excellent data storytelling through high/low precision grouping with distinct
+    colors and sizes
+  - All spec features fully implemented with correct funnel plot geometry
+  - Realistic neutral clinical meta-analysis data with clear publication bias signal
+  - Clean colorblind-safe palette (blue/orange) with refined typography
+  - Dual PNG/HTML output leverages pygal SVG nature
+  weaknesses:
+  - Minor canvas waste on left side of x-axis (xrange could be tighter)
+  - Legend text is functional but could be more refined in presentation
+  image_description: The plot displays a funnel plot for meta-analysis of 15 RCTs
+    comparing drug vs placebo. The x-axis shows "Log Odds Ratio (Effect Size)" ranging
+    from -1.10 to ~0.18, and the y-axis shows "Standard Error (precision ↑)" inverted
+    from 0 (top) to 0.30 (bottom). A solid dark navy vertical line marks the pooled
+    effect at LOR = -0.47, and a grey vertical line marks the null effect at LOR =
+    0. Dashed teal-blue diagonal lines form the funnel shape (95% pseudo CI). High-precision
+    studies appear as larger dark blue dots clustered tightly near the pooled effect
+    at the top, while low-precision studies appear as smaller orange dots scattered
+    more widely toward the bottom, with visible rightward asymmetry suggesting publication
+    bias. The background is a subtle off-white (#F8FAFB) with y-axis grid lines only.
+    The legend at the bottom shows five entries across three columns. Overall layout
+    is clean and professional.
+  criteria_checklist:
+    visual_quality:
+      score: 29
+      max: 30
+      items:
+      - id: VQ-01
+        name: Text Legibility
+        score: 8
+        max: 8
+        passed: true
+        comment: 'All font sizes explicitly set: title=48, label=28, major_label=24,
+          legend=22, value=18. All text clearly readable.'
+      - id: VQ-02
+        name: No Overlap
+        score: 6
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: No overlapping text elements. Legend entries well-spaced across 3
+          columns.
+      - id: VQ-03
+        name: Element Visibility
+        score: 6
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: Study dots clearly visible with differentiated sizing (22 vs 16).
+          Funnel and reference lines distinguishable.
+      - id: VQ-04
+        name: Color Accessibility
+        score: 4
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: Blue/orange colorblind-safe combination with excellent contrast.
+      - id: VQ-05
+        name: Layout & Canvas
+        score: 3
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: Good utilization overall. Minor wasted space on left side of x-axis.
+      - id: VQ-06
+        name: Axis Labels & Title
+        score: 2
+        max: 2
+        passed: true
+        comment: 'Descriptive labels with context: Log Odds Ratio (Effect Size) and
+          Standard Error (precision ↑).'
+    design_excellence:
+      score: 14
+      max: 20
+      items:
+      - id: DE-01
+        name: Aesthetic Sophistication
+        score: 6
+        max: 8
+        passed: true
+        comment: Strong design with custom palette, refined typography, intentional
+          color hierarchy. Clearly above defaults.
+      - id: DE-02
+        name: Visual Refinement
+        score: 4
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: Subtle plot background, y-guides only, muted gridlines, generous
+          whitespace.
+      - id: DE-03
+        name: Data Storytelling
+        score: 4
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: Two-group coloring with bias region label creates visual hierarchy.
+          Asymmetry communicates publication bias.
+    spec_compliance:
+      score: 15
+      max: 15
+      items:
+      - id: SC-01
+        name: Plot Type
+        score: 5
+        max: 5
+        passed: true
+        comment: Correct funnel plot as XY scatter with reference lines and CI boundaries.
+      - id: SC-02
+        name: Required Features
+        score: 4
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: 'All spec features present: inverted y-axis, pooled effect line,
+          null effect line, CI funnel, study points with size variation.'
+      - id: SC-03
+        name: Data Mapping
+        score: 3
+        max: 3
+        passed: true
+        comment: X=effect size, Y=standard error, y-axis correctly inverted.
+      - id: SC-04
+        name: Title & Legend
+        score: 3
+        max: 3
+        passed: true
+        comment: Title matches required format. Legend labels descriptive and accurate.
+    data_quality:
+      score: 15
+      max: 15
+      items:
+      - id: DQ-01
+        name: Feature Coverage
+        score: 6
+        max: 6
+        passed: true
+        comment: Shows high/low precision studies, visible asymmetry, studies inside/outside
+          CI, outlier present.
+      - id: DQ-02
+        name: Realistic Context
+        score: 5
+        max: 5
+        passed: true
+        comment: 15 RCTs drug vs placebo with realistic author/year names. Neutral
+          healthcare topic.
+      - id: DQ-03
+        name: Appropriate Scale
+        score: 4
+        max: 4
+        passed: true
+        comment: Log odds ratios around -0.5, SE 0.07-0.26 — realistic for clinical
+          meta-analyses.
+    code_quality:
+      score: 10
+      max: 10
+      items:
+      - id: CQ-01
+        name: KISS Structure
+        score: 3
+        max: 3
+        passed: true
+        comment: Clean linear structure with no functions or classes.
+      - id: CQ-02
+        name: Reproducibility
+        score: 2
+        max: 2
+        passed: true
+        comment: np.random.seed(42) set and data is hardcoded.
+      - id: CQ-03
+        name: Clean Imports
+        score: 2
+        max: 2
+        passed: true
+        comment: Only numpy, pygal, and Style — all used.
+      - id: CQ-04
+        name: Code Elegance
+        score: 2
+        max: 2
+        passed: true
+        comment: Clean Pythonic code with boolean masking for group separation.
+      - id: CQ-05
+        name: Output & API
+        score: 1
+        max: 1
+        passed: true
+        comment: Saves as plot.png. Also generates plot.html.
+    library_mastery:
+      score: 7
+      max: 10
+      items:
+      - id: LM-01
+        name: Idiomatic Usage
+        score: 4
+        max: 5
+        passed: true
+        comment: Good use of XY chart, Style class, per-series stroke_style/dots_size,
+          formatter, legend suppression, inverse_y_axis.
+      - id: LM-02
+        name: Distinctive Features
+        score: 3
+        max: 5
+        passed: true
+        comment: 'Uses pygal-specific: inverse_y_axis, SVG dasharray, tooltip_border_radius,
+          dual PNG/HTML output.'
+  verdict: APPROVED
+impl_tags:

+  - html-export
